text:
Once upon an old wooden plank bridge lived a little green ogre named Oggy. Oggy loved his swampy home, filled with all that smelled of swamp gas — sweetamber gumdrops, onion jam sandwiches, and, best of all, mud pudding.

One crisp autumn morning, Oggy decided he wanted to explore more of the world than just his beloved swamp. He heard tales of mysterious, ancient trees that whispered secrets, about lost kingdoms under the moonlight. Oggy packed his appetite-first pantry with all kinds of snacks for a long journey.

After saying his ogre guten appetit, Oggy set out, his belly rumbling loudly. He wandered through meadows cloaked in yellowing leaves and across woods thick with gnarled branches.

As he ventured deeper into fairy-tale land, he stumbled across an enchanted circle of stones illuminated by the light of a full moon. A figure caught his eye. She was like a dream come to life: tall and sable, her
